column2: Identifier for the molecule: International nonproprietary name of the therapeutic according to the WHO (https://www.who.int/teams/health-product-and-policy-standards/inn/inn-bio)
Primary Key- IDC unique identifier for the specific drug (thus associated to a unique trade name)
Commercial name for the drug product (if approved) or alternative name give to an unapproved drug
Organization(s) who market(ed) the therapeutic
Whether the therapeutic is a biosimilar of a parental molecule
If the therapeutic is a biosimilar or reformulation of a parental therapeutic, that parental therapeutic ID will be listed here
Free-form description of the therapeutic taken from the reference database or manufacturer
Controlled language category describing the type of biotherapeutic. Note an immunotoxin here is classified as a binder (antibody, cytokine, etc.) fused to a toxin (ex. Diptheria toxin)

Controlled language describing the most foreign species by which the sequence was derived. For example, an immunotoxin with a fully human ScFv fused to a bacterial toxin would be considered bacterial.
Controlled language describing the specific, isotype, etc. for the antibody constant regions incorproated in the therpaeutic
Controlled language for light chain containing therapeutics only, lamnda or kappa

Only relevant for Fc containing therapeutics: any noted modifications made the Fc (Note the reported sequence was not analyzed to determine if any Fc modifications were made or to variefy modifications)
Controlled language describing if any conjugates were added to the drug post expression
Free from description of the conjugate modifying the drug, if any were made
Host organism in which the genetic constructs designed to produce the protein were incorporated into
IDC unique identifier(s) for the amino acid sequence of the therapeutic that are listed in the Sequence table
A sequence is considered verified if it was found in at least two independent databases
The name of the target that the therapeutic binds to or targets to carry out its therapeutic effect
Free-form description of the therapeutics mechanism of action
Controlled language indicating the further clinical stage of evaluation that the therapeutic reached to date
Whether the therapeutic was ever approved by the FDA
Date of FDA approval for the oldest indication of the therapeutic
Whether the therapeutic was ever approved by the EU
Date of EU approval for the oldest indication of the therapeutic
Primary Key- IDC unique identifier for the protein chain sequence for the molecule
Foreign Key- IDC unique identifier for the drug representing the original parental molecule (or just the singular drug if no biosimilars or alternative formulations are in the dataset)
International nonproprietary name of the therapeutic according to the WHO (https://www.who.int/teams/health-product-and-policy-standards/inn/inn-bio)
Number of times the chain is represented in the protein complex
Identifier for the individual chain within the protein complex
Controlled language descriptor that indicated if the chain is a heavy chain, light chain or domain (and if so what number within the protein complex)
Amino acid sequence for the indicated chain
Reference link to source(s) where amino acid sequence was found
Indicates whether the row has been manually reviewed after its entry into the dataset to ensure its accurarcy. Manually reviewed rows could be considered as higher quality.
Primary Key- IDC unique identifier for the individual clinical trial
Controlled language describing the source type where the clinical trial data was acquired from
Unique identifier for the specific source of the clinical trial data

column1: Molecule Assessed for ADA INN Name
column2: The INN name of the therapeutic that is being assess for ADA in said row

column1: Therapeutic Assessed for ADA Trade name
column2: The trade name of the drug that is being assess for ADA in said row
Foreign Key- The therapeutic ID of the therapeutic that is being assess for ADA in said row
Primary Key- Unique identifier generated based on the clinical trial ID, the arm ID (e.g. A1 = Arm 1) and the datapoint ID for that arm (e.g. 001 = datapoint 1 for the indicated arm).

Indicates what the specific target was for the ADA testing (ex. For a bi-specific you could be evaluating ADA against arm 1 or arm 2, or for a PEGylated molecule you could evaluate ADA against the molecule or the PEG)
Indicates if the cohort has been exposed to the drug or not (Therapeutic Naïve vs Therapeutic Exposed). Pre-dose measures or placebo control groups would be labeled as Therapeutic Naïve.
Free-form description of the assigned intervention for the specific arm in the trial
Free-form description of the timepoint within the trial that the data is being captured
Free-form description of the dosing specifications for the therapeutic of interest and other drugs for the cohort. This could include dosing level and frequency.
Free-form description of the dosing schedule for the therapeutic which could include the dates or cadence of dosing and the number and length of treatment periods.
Controlled language indicating the broad category the cohorts disease indication falls within
Free-form description iof the disease indication(s) being evaluated
Free-form list of other drugs administered in the trial arm. May include other biologics with the Protein IDC Identifiers indicator or small molecule drugs.
Free-form description of the demographics of the patients evaluated in either the cohort or trial
Date the clinical trial was started. This could be left blank if it was not reported in the source document.
Date the clinical trial ended. This could be left blank if it was not reported in the source document.
Controlled language indicating the route of adminstration for the therapeutic within the cohort

Number of days an ADA measurement was taken post-dose of a therapeutic. Sometimes this is reported in source documents as the maximum date ADA was assessed at, thus could represent multiple earlier dates as well.

column1: Number of Patients analyzed for ADA
column2: Number of patients evaluated for ADA for the given cohort at the given timepoint
Number of patients testing postive for ADA above the threshold specificied by the parameters of the ADA assay
Number of patients testing postive for ADA divided by the total number of patients analyzed for ADA
If reported, information around the type of ADA assay used. In some entries this could also contain information about the assay sensitivity.
If reported, information around the ADA assay sensitivity and parameters by which a patient was determined to be ADA+ or ADA negative

column1: Number of patients analyzed for ADA titers
column2: Number of patients evaluated for ADA titers for the given cohort at the given timepoint
Reported average ADA titers amongst those evaluated
Indicates whether any neutralizing ADAs were reported. Sometimes the presence or absence of nADAs are the only measures reported.

column1: Number of patients analyzed for nADAs titers
column2: Number of patients evaluated for neturalizing ADA titers for the given cohort at the given timepoint
Reported average neturalizing ADA titers amongst those evaluated

column1: Number of patients analyzed for nADA
column2: Number of patients evaluated for neturalizing ADA for the given cohort at the given timepoint
Number of patients testing postive for neutralizing ADA above the threshold specificied by the parameters of the assay
Number of patients testing postive for nADA divided by the total number of patients analyzed for nADA

column1: Frequency of patients with hypersensitivity or injection site reactions
column2: Reported frequency of patients reported to have severe hypersensitivity or injection site reactions. These may or may not be correlated with ADA positivity.
Reported conclusions whether ADA was correlated or implicated in the impact of Pharmacokinetics of the therapeutic.
Reported conclusions whether ADA was correlated or implicated in the impact of efficacy of the therapeutic.
Indicating whether the trial was conducted as a prospective or retrospective study
Indicating whether the trial was randomized or not randomized in relation to the therapeutic being assessed for ADAs
Whether the trial was blinded
Indicating what type of comparator the therapeutic was assessed against (if any)
Free-form text indicating what the primary endpoints of the trial were
